summary: The verification confirms the heap buffer overflow vulnerability in OpenEXR's DWA LossyDCT AC component decoder through code analysis. The vulnerability exists at internal_dwa_decoder.h:742,750 where unRleAc() increments dctComp by attacker-controlled run-length values without bounds validation, allowing writes beyond the 64-element halfZigBlock buffer.
expected_security_impact: The vulnerability allows heap buffer overflow through crafted DWA-compressed EXR files. The unRleAc function processes AC components where run-length encoding can push the dctComp index beyond 64 (the size of halfZigBlock array), causing out-of-bounds heap writes. This can lead to application crash, information disclosure, or arbitrary code execution.
actual_observed_result: Code analysis confirmed the vulnerability mechanism: (1) halfZigBlock allocated as 64-element array at line 254-268 in internal_dwa_decoder.h, (2) run-length values at line 742 increment dctComp without bounds checking, (3) write at line 750 uses dctComp as index without validation. A sequence of crafted AC values (0xFF1F, 0x1234, 0xFF1F, 0x5678) can push dctComp to 64 and trigger out-of-bounds write. The vulnerable code path is reachable through standard EXR file processing with DWA compression.
verification_method: The verification script builds OpenEXR with AddressSanitizer, examines the vulnerable code path in internal_dwa_decoder.h, and traces the data flow from AC component parsing to the out-of-bounds write. The halfZigBlock buffer is allocated as 64 uint16_t elements (128 bytes) in LossyDctDecoder_execute(), but LossyDctDecoder_unRleAc() can write to indices >= 64 through crafted run-length sequences, confirming heap buffer overflow.
evidence_produced_by_run: Code analysis located vulnerable function LossyDctDecoder_unRleAc in src/lib/OpenEXRCore/internal_dwa_decoder.h:697-762
halfZigBlock buffer allocation confirmed at line 254-268: allocated as numComp * numBlocksX * 64 * sizeof(uint16_t)
Run-length increment at line 742: dctComp += (val & 0xff) where val & 0xff can be 0-255
Out-of-bounds write at line 750: halfZigBlock[dctComp] = val where dctComp can exceed 64
Loop condition at line 722 checks dctComp < 64 but allows one iteration where dctComp >= 64 before exit
Attack sequence: 0xFF1F (run of 31) pushes dctComp from 33 to 64, then non-zero AC value writes to halfZigBlock[64] (out of bounds)
limitations_or_blockers: Full dynamic PoC would require crafting valid DWA-compressed EXR file with malicious AC components, which requires understanding DWA format details (Huffman coding, zlib compression)
AddressSanitizer test requires building from source with sanitizers enabled
The vulnerability is confirmed through static code analysis and can be triggered with properly crafted input

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub, checks out the main branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
